description: India Book III, 39: Unfinished draft of a letter from Avraham Ibn Yiju, in Aydhab, to a member of Khalaf b. Bundār's family in Aden, probably around 1152, while Ibn Yijū was on his way to Egypt. Ibn Yijū had left Aden suddenly with Sitt al-Dār, and he broke off her engagement with Khalaf b. Bundār's son in Aden. His sudden departure must have caused distrust and fallout. It is possible that Ibn Yijū hid his reasons for departing suddenly, and that this secrecy and haste broke the relationships of mutual trust between families/business partners. In this letter, Ihe assures the recipient that he did not flee because of a debt. The debt he owed is too small to talk about, he says, and he will pay it in person if he returns to Yemen, but he also invites the recipient to collect its value from some of Ibn Yijū's goods in a warehouse in Aden. Meanwhile, Sitt al-Dār has been crying day and night because she misses her life in Aden, where she had lived for three years with Khalaf b. Bundār's family. Ibn Yijū did not finish writing this letter and never sent it, at least not in its present guise. (Information from Goitein and Friedman, India Traders) PG
description_he: מכתב ביהודית-ערבית מהמאה ה-12 מ-ʿAydhāb. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
